_comment: Copy this file into registry/servers/<your-server>/manifest.json and replace the placeholder values.
name: my-server
description: Example MCP server manifest showing the full registry format.
_comment_author: Author identifies the original creator of the server package.
_comment_maintainer: Maintainer identifies who answers review follow-up and keeps the manifest fresh.
_comment_trust: Trust metadata helps users understand support level and lifecycle state.
_comment_transport: List every transport you support and choose one default for clients.
_comment_install: Set package managers you support and use null when a channel does not exist.
_comment_stdio: stdio is the most portable transport for local MCP clients.
_comment_http: Use {port} in args when the launch command needs the bound port value.
_comment_unix: Use {socket_path} or {name} when the server can bind to a Unix socket.
_comment_sse: SSE endpoints are useful when a server already exposes a long-lived event stream.
_comment_tools: Describe every tool in plain language so clients can render them well.

tools.name: my_server_search
tools.description: Search records managed by the example server.

tools.name: my_server_create_record
tools.description: Create a new record in the example service.

tools.name: my_server_get_status
tools.description: Return the current status and health summary.

_comment_credentials: Each credential maps to one environment variable that users can set.

credentials.description: API key used to authenticate to My Server.
Workspace slug or tenant identifier for multi-tenant deployments.

_comment_discovery: Tags drive search filtering; categories group the server by broader workflow.
_comment_hive: The hive block is optional and only needed for Hive-aware clients.
